untranslatable_reason: Signal requires tracking implied volatility (IV) changes and option Greeks (theta, vega) which are not available in the allowed indicator list. The core mechanics—selling near-term and buying longer-term options at same strike with profit from IV decay or price moves—cannot be fully replicated with price/technical indicators alone. Used hvol_20 as a proxy for volatility environment, but this does not capture the directional option spread P&L accurately.
translation_notes: Reverse calendar spreads are inherently options strategies requiring IV tracking and time decay analysis. The signal describes profit from IV drop (vega positive long-term leg, vega negative short-term leg) and directional moves at expiration. Without access to IV levels, implied volatility surface, or option pricing indicators, the entry/exit conditions are approximations only. Recommend implementing this signal in an options-specific backtest engine with IV data rather than a price-action engine.
